questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 6-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root locus plots:
options: Paths of closed-loop poles as gain varies from 0 to ∞
questions.solution: Root locus: K > 0 (standard) or K < 0 (complementary). jω-axis crossing = marginal stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 5 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Microstrip line characteristic impedance increases with:
questions.solution: Z₀ ∝ h/W. Higher h → higher Z₀. Higher ε_r → lower Z₀.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 50 has a base current of 10 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The mass action law:
options: n·p = n_i² (product of electron and hole concentrations is constant at equilibrium)
questions.solution: n·p = n_i² at thermal equilibrium. For N-type: n ≈ N_D, p = n_i²/N_D.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPU architecture — Von Neumann:
options: Single memory for data + instructions. Sequential access. Harvard: separate busses
questions.solution: Von Neumann: one bus → bottleneck. Harvard: faster (simultaneous). Modified Harvard: modern ARM.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Flyback converter: energy transfer occurs during:
questions.solution: Flyback: energy stored in transformer during ON, transferred to output during OFF. Isolated buck-boost topology.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 20 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Matrix converter: key characteristic is:
options: Direct AC-AC conversion without DC energy storage
questions.solution: Matrix converter: bidirectional switches connect any input phase to any output phase. No DC link capacitor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radix-2 FFT length must be:
questions.solution: Radix-2: N=2^m. Decimation-in-time (DIT) or decimation-in-frequency (DIF). Butterfly structure.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Gallium Nitride (GaN) power devices advantage over Silicon:
options: Lower switching losses at high frequency
questions.solution: GaN: wider bandgap, faster switching, lower losses. Higher frequency operation. Better than Si for MHz-range converters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Period of signal x(t)=cos(2π·3t)+sin(2π·5t):
questions.solution: GCD of frequencies: GCD(3,5)=1. Period = 1/1 = 1 s.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Three-phase to single-phase cycloconverter:
options: Converts 3-phase AC to variable frequency single-phase AC
questions.solution: Cycloconverter: direct AC-AC conversion. Variable frequency without DC link. Low frequency range. High power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reflection coefficient at boundary between media 1 and 2:
questions.solution: Γ = (Z₂-Z₁)/(Z₂+Z₁). For Z₂>Z₁: Γ>0. Short: Γ=-1. Open: Γ=+1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boolean algebra was introduced by:
questions.solution: Boolean algebra: binary variables + AND, OR, NOT operations. Basis of digital logic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 8051 microcontroller:
options: 8-bit. 4 KB ROM, 128 B RAM, 4×8-bit ports, 2 timers, UART. 40-pin DIP
questions.solution: 8051: Intel. 12 clocks/machine cycle. 4 register banks. Bit-addressable memory. Upward compatible.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Skin depth δ:
options: Depth where current density falls to 1/e of surface value. δ = 1/√(πfμσ)
questions.solution: Skin effect: AC current flows near surface. Higher f → smaller δ. Cu at 60 Hz: δ ≈ 8.5 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 8-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARM — CPSR register:
options: Current Program Status Register. Condition flags (N, Z, C, V), mode bits, interrupt disable
questions.solution: CPSR: N=negative, Z=zero, C=carry/borrow, V=overflow. SPSR: saved CPSR on exception.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 600 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Serial communication — I²C:
options: Synchronous. 2 wires (SDA, SCL). Addressing (7/10 bit). Multi-master. Acknowledge
questions.solution: I²C: open-drain. Pull-up resistors. 100 kHz (standard), 400 kHz (fast). Each device has address.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.solution: 8086: 16-bit data, 20-bit address via segment:offset. 1MB memory, 64KB I/O.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dipole antenna radiation pattern is:
questions.solution: Half-wave dipole: omnidirectional in H-plane, figure-8 in E-plane. Gain ≈ 2.15 dBi.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Convolution of x(t) with δ(t-T):
questions.solution: sifting property: x(t)*δ(t-T) = x(t-T). Shifts signal by T.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 900 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Companding in PCM:
options: Compresses dynamic range before quantizing (μ-law, A-law)
questions.solution: Companding: more quantization levels for small signals (higher SNR for low amplitudes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LTI system H(z) with poles inside unit circle is:
questions.solution: BIBO stable: all poles inside unit circle (|z|<1).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PSK bit error rate: P_e = 0.5·erfc(√(E_b/N₀)). For BER=10⁻⁶:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parseval's theorem relates:
options: Energy in time domain to energy in frequency domain

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DTFT of rectangular pulse x[n]=1 for 0≤n≤N-1:
questions.solution: X(ω) = Σ_{n=0}^{N-1} e^{-jωn} = (1-e^{-jωN})/(1-e^{-jω}). Simplifies to Dirichlet kernel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 14 kHz tone is sampled at 10 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |14 − 10| = 4 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 8-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Observer-based control: separation principle states:
options: Controller and observer can be designed independently
questions.solution: Separation: total controller = state feedback gain + observer. Eigenvalues of combined system = union of individual sets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A high common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR) in a differential amplifier means it:
options: Strongly rejects signals common to both inputs
questions.solution: CMRR = differential gain/common-mode gain; high CMRR suppresses noise common to both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RC low-pass: R=2.2kΩ, C=0.01μF. -3dB frequency:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=470kΩ, R₁=2.2kΩ, Vin=2V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp slew rate:
options: Maximum rate of output voltage change. SR = dVout/dt max. V/us
questions.solution: Slew rate: limitation for large signals. Full-power bandwidth: f_max = SR/(2π·V_peak).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for wider bandwidth, lower distortion and better stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT bias: Vcc=5V, Rb=330kΩ, Rc=2.2kΩ, β=150. Base current Ib:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta modulation (DM):
options: Transmits 1-bit per sample (up/down step based on signal slope)
questions.solution: DM: simple, low rate. Slope overload (step too small for fast signal). Granular noise (step too large for flat signal).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=100kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=1V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 100 µH and C = 1000 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: VCO phase noise: how does increasing tank Q affect it:
questions.solution: Higher Q tank = narrower bandwidth = better filtering of noise = lower phase noise. Key design tradeoff.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An antenna is a device that:
options: Converts between guided signals and radiated EM waves
questions.solution: Antennas transduce between currents on a conductor and free-space electromagnetic waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 47 kΩ and Rin = 1 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel — shadow fading:
options: Log-normal. Obstructions create random variation. σ = 4-12 dB (environment dependent)
questions.solution: Shadowing: slow fading. Margins in link budget. Log-normal distribution. Location variability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 10 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zener diode regulator:
options: Reverse breakdown. Vout = Vz (constant). Rs limits current
questions.solution: Zener: Vz at Iz. Line regulation: ΔVout/ΔVin. Load regulation: ΔVout/ΔI_L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — input offset voltage:
options: DC voltage between inputs to force output to zero. V_os (μV-mV)
questions.solution: V_os: caused by transistor mismatch. Null offset with potentiometer. Temp drift.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT: Vcc=15V, Rb=220kΩ, β=200. Collector current Ic:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Full-wave rectifier ripple frequency is:
questions.solution: Full-wave rectifier doubles the input frequency in ripple.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A full-wave rectifier output frequency:
options: 2 × input frequency (100 Hz for 50 Hz input)
questions.solution: Full-wave: 2 pulses per cycle → f_out = 2f_in. Half-wave: f_out = f_in.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Current mirror:
options: Copies current (output = input). Widlar, Wilson, cascode variants
questions.solution: Basic mirror: Iout = Iref (matched transistors). Wilson: higher output resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VCO (Voltage Controlled Oscillator):
options: Output frequency ∝ input voltage. 555 timer, LM566, VCO in PLL
questions.solution: VCO: tuning voltage. f_out = k·V_ctrl. VCO gain K_v (Hz/V). Used in PLL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=33.0kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=0.1V. Vout:

questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=100kΩ, R₁=4.7kΩ. Closed-loop gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Hartley oscillator uses:
options: LC tank with tapped inductor (inductive divider feedback)
questions.solution: Hartley: L₁+L₂+M across C. Feedback ratio = L₂/L₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 555 timer — astable mode:
options: Free-running oscillator. f = 1.44/((R_A+2R_B)C). Duty cycle = R_B/(R_A+2R_B)
questions.solution: 555: astable (oscillator), monostable (one-shot), bistable (SR flip-flop). 8-pin timer IC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a Zener diode regulator is correct?
options: Its voltage rises steeply with current in breakdown
It must be reverse biased below breakdown
It maintains a nearly constant voltage in reverse breakdown
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener voltage stays almost constant over a range of current.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Kelvin connection: eliminates error due to:
questions.solution: Kelvin (4-wire): separate current and voltage leads. Voltage measured across DUT, not including lead drops.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Circular convolution vs linear: circular wraps around, to get linear via:
options: Zero-pad both sequences to length L+M-1
questions.solution: Linear convolution of length-L and length-M needs L+M-1 points. Zero-padding before DFT multiplication avoids time-domain aliasing.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: DTC (direct torque control): controls:
options: Torque and flux directly without current loops
questions.solution: DTC: estimates torque and flux from measured V and I. Hysteresis controllers select voltage vectors directly.
